examples.title: Sending a Message to a Smart Contract
examples.description: This pseudocode demonstrates how to send a message to a smart contract using the `eth_sendTransaction` method.
examples.code:
FUNCTION send_message(contract_address, message_data, gas_allocated)
 TRANSACTION = create_transaction(contract_address, message_data, gas_allocated)
 eth_sendTransaction(TRANSACTION)
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode defines a function `send_message` that takes the contract address, message data, and gas allocated as input. It creates a transaction using the `create_transaction` function and sends it to the Ethereum network using the `eth_sendTransaction` method.

examples.title: Message Call Transaction in Solidity
examples.description: This Solidity code demonstrates how to execute a message call transaction using the `call` function.
examples.explanation: This Solidity code defines a contract `MessageCall` that has a function `callMessage` which takes the contract address and message data as input. It executes a message call transaction using the `call` function.
